response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates valuable geospatial domain expertise and a proven ability to master new technologies, his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is limited to 2007–2008, and his 15+ year career has focused on web and full-stack development rather than systems programming or data pipelines. For a mid-senior IC role where modern C++ (or Rust) is the primary requirement, the technical foundation gap is too significant to overcome, despite his strengths in distributed systems thinking and domain knowledge.
strengths: Deep geospatial expertise from founding GISCollective and building OpenGreenMap GIS platform, directly applicable to navigation and routing systems
Proven track record architecting large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby with low-latency optimization skills
Consistent pattern of rapidly acquiring new languages and technical domains across embedded, web, mobile, and desktop systems
concerns: C++ experience dormant since 2008; no evidence of modern C++ development or contemporary systems programming practices in 18-year gap
Career trajectory strongly emphasizes JavaScript/TypeScript web stacks and full-stack generalism rather than systems-level or high-performance data pipeline work
No Rust experience as alternative to C++, and no recent evidence of low-latency, real-time data processing at scale comparable to production routing infrastructure
